eth: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ethiopian Sign Language.
ethene: noun. The organic chemical compound ethylene.
gee: interjection. A general exclamation of surprise or frustration.
gen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Geman Deng.
gene: noun. A theoretical unit of heredity of living organisms which may take several values and (in principle) predetermines a precise trait of
genet: noun. Any of several Old World nocturnal, carnivorous mammals, of the genus Genetta, most of which have a spotted coat and a long, ringed
genie: noun. A jinn, a being descended from the jann, normally invisible to the human eye, but who may also appear in animal or human form.
ghee: noun. A type of clarified butter used in South Asian cooking.
gie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guébie.
gin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hinukh.

thee: pronoun. Objective and reflexive case of thou.

thin: adjective. Having little thickness or extent from one surface to its opposite.

thing: noun. That which is considered to exist as a separate entity, object, quality or concept.

ing: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Deg Xinag.
